name: Black Bean Flautas
recipeIngredient: 2 cans black beans, including liquid (16 oz., each)
2 teaspoons chili powder, or to taste
recipeInstructions:
1. In a medium saucepan, combine beans, bean liquid, chili powder, cumin, garlic, and bay leaf. Simmer for 5 minutes.
2. Remove the bay leaf; discard it.
3. Drain the bean mixture, reserving the liquid as needed for desired consistency. Mash the beans, adding back liquid as needed to achieve a thick puree.
4. Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
5. Place 1 or 2 heaping tablespoons of bean mixture in the center of each tortilla. Spread out slightly with the back of a spoon.
6. Top each tortilla with some green onions, tomatoes, and cheese.
7. Roll up each tortilla into a tube shape, seam side down. Continue until all tortillas have been used.
8. Place the rolled tortillas in a 9x13-inch baking pan.
9. Bake for about 5 minutes, or until lightly browned.
10. Use leftover bean mixture for dipping, or dip in salsa, sour cream, or yogurt. Makes 12 flautas.
Variation: For an even faster version, substitute canned refried beans for mashed black beans and spices. Spread the beans on tortillas and proceed with the recipe. Per flauta: 175 calories, 5 grams fat, 3 grams fiber.
